notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for finland. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

chunks.title: Chinese passport tourism route for Finland
chunks.content: Chinese ordinary passport holders planning a short tourism trip to Finland should normally use the Finland/Schengen short-stay Type C tourism route when Finland is the only destination, the longest-stay destination, or the main purpose destination. VIZA AI should first confirm nationality, legal residence, destination set, stay length, day split across Schengen countries, first entry if stays are equal, purpose, passport validity, travel medical insurance, accommodation, itinerary, funds, return ties and biometrics. Do not route this trip to US, UK, Vietnam, Indonesia or another non-Schengen visa.

chunks.title: Finland Schengen where-to-apply and documents
chunks.content: EU Schengen guidance says a short-stay Schengen visa covers temporary visits of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. For a single-country trip, apply to the consulate/official visa channel of that country. For multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time or, if stays are equal, the first-entry country. Applications should normally be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey. Common documents include passport, signed Schengen application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance, itinerary/accommodation, funds, evidence of return intention, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days. Use Finland's official consular or outsourced visa application channel for the applicant's legal residence country before booking an appointment. EU common source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en

documents.title: Finland Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Finland application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Finland consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Finland is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://um.fi/visa-to-visit-finland. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://um.fi/visa-to-visit-finland. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Finland fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Finland supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for finland.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://um.fi/visa-to-visit-finland ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen-borders-and-visa/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf

Passport and identity fields:
- Passport and other travel documents – Finnish citizens'
- Passport application by persons liable for military service
- Even if you did not need a visa to visit Finland, you need a valid passport or some other travel document accepted by Finland.

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- It must be valid for at least ninety days after your intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Make sure your passport is valid for at least three (3) months after the end of your intended travel and that it has been issued no later than ten (10) years ago.
- Your travel insurance must be in force for the period of the validity of the applied visa and cover the entire Schengen area.

Travel plan and port fields:
- A Schengen visa is an entry permit for a short, temporary visit of less than 90 days in any 180-day period.
- It must be valid for at least ninety days after your intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Upon arrival in Finland, you must meet the general entry requirements according to which a person must not constitute a danger to public order and security, national health, or Finland’s international relations.

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- Examples of such documents are tickets, a confirmation of hotel reservation, a private invitation letter and an official invitation.
- The invitation is a free-form letter indicating the host organisation’s or private host’s contact details and the invited person's details, such as name, date of birth, address, passport number, and purpose and length of visit.
- Please note that an invitation does not guarantee that a visa will be issued.

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- The mission may request that you append certain documents to the application to show the purpose, conditions and duration of the visit.
- The invitation is a free-form letter indicating the host organisation’s or private host’s contact details and the invited person's details, such as name, date of birth, address, passport number, and purpose and length of visit.
- In addition to the above, you may be asked to provide information concerning the itinerary, means of transport, return trip, financial means of support and assets and employment.

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- Do you suspect misuse of development cooperation funds?
- Your travel insurance must be in force for the period of the validity of the applied visa and cover the entire Schengen area.
- The minimum coverage of the policy must be EUR 30,000 and the insurance must cover expenses arising from a sudden illness and accident (also the patient's repatriation) and repatriation expenses in case of death.

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
